prerequisites: A Statsig account with access to a project at console.statsig.com.

A Console API Key created in the project's settings. Console API keys (Statsig CAPI keys) can read all project configuration and create, update, and delete entities, so treat the key as a privileged secret.
A start date and an end date defining the window of data to extract (the required start_date and end_date config fields).

authMethods.name: Console API Key (STATSIG-API-KEY header)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the single api_key secret field. Statsig's Console API requires every request to include a STATSIG-API-KEY header whose value is a Console API Key. Generate the key in Project Settings on console.statsig.com (the API keys page, under Environments & Keys) and supply it as api_key. Do not use a Client SDK key or Server Secret key here; the Console API requires a Console API Key.

setupSteps.title: Create a Console API Key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to console.statsig.com and open the project you want to extract from. Go to Project Settings and open the API keys page (Environments & Keys) at console.statsig.com/api_keys. Create a Console API Key. Console API keys are the most powerful keys in a project, so generate one specifically for this integration and store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key
setupSteps.body: Set the api_key config field to the Console API Key you created. The connector sends it as the STATSIG-API-KEY header that the Statsig Console API requires on every request.

setupSteps.title: Set the extraction date window
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date and end_date config fields to bound the period of data the connector pulls from the Statsig Console API.

sources.title: Statsig Console API - Introduction (STATSIG-API-KEY header, key creation)
Statsig API Keys (Console API key type and management)
